steps.title: Locate the decisive contrast
steps.content: The strongest signal is the “though” structure: smaller areas experienced less congestion than very large cities, yet congestion still worsened in those smaller areas. Supporting data must show both the severity difference and the upward trend.

steps.title: Test the answer against the data
steps.content:
The correct answer shows that Boise's delays were always lower than NYC's for each year between 1990 and 2000, and that both areas experienced rising delays during this decade.

The other choices either omit evidence of worsening in smaller areas or contradict the graph data.

That confirms B is correct.
